name: Platform Policy Pack and Change Governance

phase_boundary.scope: Decision and policy source-of-truth work only.

ticket_quality_rule: Autonomous execution is not the problem. Stale, shallow, under-specified, or non-predictive tickets are the problem. Implementation is not stale by itself; stale tickets are the risk. Phase 5A tickets must be fresh against current merged main, predictive, dependency-aware, validation-backed, exact-file-plan-driven, and must express maximum concrete policy capability within the approved Phase 5A scope.

tickets.title: Baseline Phase 5A controls, current-state inventory, and roadmap housekeeping
tickets.objective: Confirm Phase 5A starts from current merged main, finalize the current-state inventory, and update stale roadmap/current-state summaries without starting Phase 5A execution outputs beyond approved housekeeping.
scope: Inspect current merged main and Phase 5A planning basis.
Inventory existing ADRs, standards, contracts, manifests, screen contracts, Prisma source of truth, generated registry, Phase 4B audit, and Phase 5A readiness handoff.
Update stale roadmap/current-state summaries only where explicitly approved by this ticket.
Record the Phase 5A authority map and policy output inventory.
non_scope: Do not create policy pack sections beyond inventory and housekeeping.
Do not create ADR-0015 through ADR-0018.
Do not implement Foundry, runtime policy engine, module installer, business modules, production auth, deployment, secrets, or runtime services.
required_outputs: Current-state inventory evidence artifact.
Authority map showing source-of-truth hierarchy and Phase 5A policy output targets.
Stale roadmap/current-state housekeeping changes, if exact-file planning approves them.
tests_required: Documentation/content validation for stale roadmap references.
Scope scan confirming no runtime, Prisma, package, generated registry, ADR-0015 through ADR-0018, policy pack, or Foundry files were created.
stop_conditions: Housekeeping requires runtime, Prisma, package, generated registry, or contract changes.
AGENTS.md update would become a roadmap rewrite instead of narrow current-state cleanup.
Phase 5B, Foundry, runtime, module, production auth, deployment, secrets, or business-module scope appears.
Exact-file plan exceeds stale current-state housekeeping.
acceptance_criteria: Current-state summaries identify Phase 4B as merged and Phase 5A as next.
Authority map references the canonical Phase 5A planning basis.
No Phase 5A execution outputs such as ADRs, policy pack docs, or standards are created in this ticket.
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert only P5A-000 housekeeping and evidence artifacts; preserve the approved Phase 5A planning basis and ticket pack.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if stale roadmap cleanup requires broader roadmap restructuring, source-of-truth hierarchy changes, or non-documentation changes.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Reconfirm main HEAD and Phase 4B merge state before edits. Tickets become stale when they assume old roadmap status.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: A repo-grounded inventory artifact and, if needed, narrow current-state housekeeping changes showing Phase 5A starts from Phase 4B-merged main.
tickets.split_if: Split if exact-file planning shows more than four roadmap/current-state files need substantive restructuring or if AGENTS.md changes exceed stale Current Project State lines.
requires_human_approval_if: Any source-of-truth hierarchy change is needed.
Any runtime, schema, contract, registry, package, or dependency change is proposed.
decision_options: Narrow roadmap/current-state housekeeping only.
Stop and request approval for broader roadmap rewrite.

tickets.title: Core update, module definition, and ownership policy
tickets.objective: Define platform change governance, valid module definition, module ownership, review authority, and breaking-change classification for the consolidated Platform Policy Pack.
scope: Produce Policy 1: Core Update and Platform Change Policy.
Produce Policy 2: Module Definition and Ownership Policy.
Define enforcement points and Phase 5B implementation inputs for both policies.
Align prose with module manifest schema and existing ADRs.
non_scope: Do not create runtime module loading or Foundry behavior.
Do not implement business modules or platform change tooling.
required_outputs: Policy 1 with change categories, breaking-change definition, approvals, evidence requirements, and enforcement points.
Policy 2 with module identity, ownership, source-of-truth, ownership transfer, and review authority rules.
Phase 5B input notes for Foundry validation and policy enforcement.
tests_required: Policy coverage check for Policies 1 and 2.
validation_commands: rg "Foundry runtime|module installer implementation|business module implementation" docs/policies/AKTI_ERP_Platform_Policy_Pack_v1.md || true
stop_conditions: Policy requires changing module-manifest.schema.ts without an explicit approved ticket.
Policy attempts to implement Foundry or module installer behavior.
Policy cannot name an enforcement point.
Policy contradicts existing module manifest fields without a superseding ADR requirement.
acceptance_criteria: Policies 1 and 2 are complete enough for Phase 5B to implement validation and review gates.
Each policy names enforcement point, evidence, owner, and Phase 5B input.
No runtime implementation is introduced.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-001 define core and module ownership policies
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert only Policy 1/2 sections and ticket artifacts; preserve prior Phase 5A baseline artifacts.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if module ownership cannot be defined without changing contracts or adding runtime authority tables.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Re-read module-manifest.schema.ts before writing; schema fields are the structural baseline.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Policy pack contains usable Policies 1 and 2 with enforcement points and Phase 5B implementation inputs.
tickets.split_if: Split if core change governance and module ownership each require more than one substantial policy section or separate ADR-level decisions.
requires_human_approval_if: New source-of-truth hierarchy is proposed.
Contract/schema changes are required.
Ownership model cannot be expressed without runtime implementation.
decision_options: Central Platform Core ownership for module definition approvals.
Module owner plus Platform Core dual approval.
Stop for approval if ownership authority cannot be derived from existing governance.

tickets.title: Module lifecycle, install/update, disable/uninstall, rollback, and evidence policies
tickets.objective: Define the non-runtime policy contract for module lifecycle, installation/update, disable/uninstall, rollback, and evidence packages.
scope: Produce Policy 3: Module Lifecycle Policy.
Produce Policy 4: Module Installation and Update Policy.
Produce Policy 13: Disable, Uninstall and Rollback Policy.
Produce Policy 14: Evidence and Audit Package Policy.
Define lifecycle states and evidence requirements that Phase 5B Foundry must implement later.
non_scope: Do not implement install, update, disable, uninstall, rollback, or Foundry lifecycle runtime.
Do not add tables, migrations, jobs, package dependencies, or module installer code.
required_outputs: Lifecycle state machine policy.
Install/update policy with evidence, compatibility, approval, and rollback triggers.
Disable/uninstall/rollback policy with data-retention and user-impact rules.
Standard module evidence/audit package policy.
stop_conditions: Lifecycle policy requires runtime installer behavior.
Policy cannot define data-retention or evidence rules without product approval.
Rollback policy implies destructive migrations.
Foundry implementation details replace policy requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Policies 3, 4, 13, and 14 are concrete and enforcement-ready for Phase 5B.
Every lifecycle state has entry, exit, evidence, and enforcement expectations.
No runtime implementation or destructive migration is authorized.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-002 define module lifecycle and evidence policies
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert Policy 3/4/13/14 sections and artifacts only; keep prior policies intact.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if lifecycle states conflict with existing manifest disable_behavior or degraded_mode_behavior.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Re-read current module manifest before defining lifecycle states; later schema changes may make old lifecycle assumptions stale.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Policy pack contains lifecycle, install/update, disable/uninstall/rollback, and evidence policies with Phase 5B enforcement inputs.
tickets.split_if: Split if install/update and disable/rollback each require separate ADR-level decisions or if evidence policy cannot be finalized in the same commit unit.
requires_human_approval_if: Destructive rollback behavior is proposed.
Data deletion or retention policy cannot be decided from existing governance.
Runtime installer work appears necessary.
decision_options: Lifecycle states: draft/proposed/certified/installed/degraded/deprecated/disabled/retired.
Minimal lifecycle states only: proposed/certified/installed/disabled.
Stop for approval if lifecycle semantics affect production data retention.
selection_criteria: Keeps runtime implementation out of Phase 5A.
Protects tenant data and audit evidence.

tickets.title: Capability, permission, menu, screen, settings, and Gatekeeper policy
tickets.objective: Define capability, permission, menu/navigation, screen registration, settings registration, and Gatekeeper preflight/approval policies for future Foundry enforcement.
scope: Produce Policy 5: Capability, Permission and Access Policy.
Produce Policy 6: Menu, Navigation and Screen Registration Policy.
Produce Policy 7: Settings and Configuration Registration Policy.
Produce Policy 8: Gatekeeper Preflight and Approval Policy.
Define enforcement points across Access Core, Gatekeeper, module manifests, screen contracts, and Phase 5B Foundry.
non_scope: Do not modify Access Core or Gatekeeper runtime behavior.
Do not implement dynamic menu/settings registration.
required_outputs: Capability naming, ownership, inheritance, and consumption policy.
Menu/navigation/screen registration policy aligned with ADR-0013.
Settings/configuration namespace and default-value policy.
Gatekeeper preflight/approval trigger and evidence policy.
tests_required: Policy coverage review for Policies 5, 6, 7, and 8.
validation_commands: rg "dynamic menu runtime|seed capability|modify screen-contract|Gatekeeper runtime" docs/policies/AKTI_ERP_Platform_Policy_Pack_v1.md || true
stop_conditions: Policy requires new runtime capability seeding before ADR-0016.
Policy requires screen-contract schema changes.
Policy cannot define Gatekeeper enforcement points.
Menu/settings policy drifts into Foundry registration implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Policies 5 through 8 are complete enough for Phase 5B Foundry and Gatekeeper implementation planning.
Policy 6 defines menu, route, capability, module_id, visibility, and enabled/disabled state concepts.
Policy 8 names Gatekeeper checks, evidence, denial/approval outcomes, and high-risk action handling.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-003 define capability registration and Gatekeeper policies
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert Policies 5/6/7/8 sections and ticket artifacts only; preserve lifecycle and ownership policies.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if capability policy requires runtime seeding, screen-contract schema edits, or Gatekeeper code changes.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: P5A-008 depends on this policy. If this ticket changes capability concepts, revalidate P5A-008 before execution.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Policy pack contains Policies 5, 6, 7, and 8 with enforcement points, manifest/screen-contract alignment, and Phase 5B inputs.
tickets.split_if: Split if capability/access policy and Gatekeeper policy each require independent ADR-level decisions or if screen registration needs schema design beyond policy prose.
requires_human_approval_if: New capability naming convention conflicts with existing capabilities.
Policy requires screen-contract schema changes.
Policy requires runtime Access Core or Gatekeeper changes.
decision_options: Capability-driven registration through module manifests and screen contracts.
Session-gated shell exception with capability-gated module regions.
Stop for approval if capability and session models cannot be reconciled in policy.
selection_criteria: Aligns with existing Access Core and Gatekeeper contracts.

tickets.title: Tenant/RLS, migration/schema contribution, and cross-module data policy
tickets.objective: Resolve tenant/RLS strategy and define migration/schema contribution plus data ownership and cross-module access policy without changing Prisma or migrations.
scope: Create ADR-0015 for tenant/RLS enforcement strategy.
Produce Policy 9: Migration and Schema Contribution Policy.
Produce Policy 10: Data Ownership, Tenant Boundary and Cross-Module Access Policy.
Define Phase 5B and Phase 6 requirements for module migrations and tenant isolation.
non_scope: Do not create migrations or RLS SQL.
Do not implement cross-module data access runtime.
required_outputs: ADR-0015 with options, decision, consequences, non-scope, and Phase 5B/Phase 6 implications.
Migration/schema contribution policy.
Tenant boundary and cross-module data access policy.
tests_required: Policy coverage review for Policies 9 and 10.
stop_conditions: ADR cannot choose service-level, DB-level, hybrid, or explicit bounded deferral.
Any Prisma schema, migration, or generated registry change is needed.
Policy would authorize destructive migrations.
Cross-module data policy cannot name enforcement points.
acceptance_criteria: ADR-0015 closes or explicitly bounds the ADR-0009 RLS deferral.
Policies 9 and 10 define module migration requirements and tenant/cross-module access rules.
No Prisma, migration, generated registry, or runtime code changes occur.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-004 decide tenant RLS and data policies
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert ADR-0015, Policy 9/10 sections, and ticket artifacts only; preserve earlier policy sections.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if tenant strategy requires runtime proof, schema changes, or migration examples beyond standards prose.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: RLS policy is high impact; re-read ADR-0009 and Prisma before deciding.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: ADR-0015 and Policies 9/10 define tenant/RLS, migration, and cross-module data rules with no Prisma or migration changes.
tickets.split_if: Split if ADR-0015 cannot be completed with Policies 9/10 in one commit unit or if RLS strategy needs separate human decision.
requires_human_approval_if: DB-level RLS requires immediate schema/migration proof.
Tenant isolation strategy cannot be selected.
Any destructive migration or runtime enforcement code is proposed.
decision_options: Service-level tenant enforcement only.
DB-level RLS for tenant-scoped module tables.
Hybrid service-level plus DB-level RLS for selected high-risk tables.
Explicit bounded deferral with Phase 6 blocker.
selection_criteria: Compatible with existing Prisma and ADR-0009.
Does not require immediate runtime implementation.
Provides clear Phase 6 module migration rules.

tickets.title: Adapter, secrets, configuration, and external dependency policy
tickets.objective: Define adapter, external dependency, secrets, and configuration safety policy for future modules and integrations without creating adapters or accessing secrets.
scope: Produce Policy 11: Adapter and External Dependency Policy.
Produce Policy 16: Security, Secrets and Configuration Safety Policy.
Define credential, retry, failure isolation, audit, and configuration registration rules.
Preserve WhatsApp protected timeline and no-production-secret boundaries.
non_scope: Do not access or create secrets.
required_outputs: Adapter/external dependency policy.
Secrets/configuration safety policy.
Phase 5B implementation notes for adapter declaration and secret validation.
tests_required: Policy coverage review for Policies 11 and 16.
stop_conditions: Policy requires accessing real secrets or credentials.
Policy requires adding external dependencies.
Policy starts real adapter implementation.
WhatsApp production behavior appears.
acceptance_criteria: Policies 11 and 16 define adapter and secret/configuration governance.
No secrets are accessed, created, exposed, or committed.
No adapter runtime is implemented.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-005 define adapter and secrets policies
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert Policies 11/16 sections and ticket artifacts only; preserve prior policy sections.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if policy requires specific external provider semantics not present in current docs.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Reconfirm no real adapter approvals were introduced after Phase 4B.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Policy pack contains adapter/external dependency and secrets/configuration policies with no secret access and no runtime adapter implementation.
tickets.split_if: Split if adapter governance and secret/configuration safety require separate policy decisions or provider-specific approvals.
requires_human_approval_if: Production credentials are needed.
A real external adapter is proposed.
Dependency installation is proposed.
decision_options: Generic adapter policy only.
Generic policy plus WhatsApp-specific protected reference.
Stop for approval if provider-specific rules are required.

tickets.title: Event schema, evidence package, observability, and SLO standards

scope: Create Event Schema Standard.
Produce Policy 17: Observability, Health and SLO Policy.

required_outputs: Event Schema Standard with minimum envelope fields.
Observability, health, and SLO policy.
Phase 5B input notes for event validation, evidence capture, and health aggregation.
stop_conditions: Standard requires runtime Zod schema implementation.
Standard requires module-manifest schema changes.
Observability policy requires runtime metrics or scheduler implementation.
Event semantics conflict with existing module event declarations.
acceptance_criteria: Event Schema Standard defines minimum envelope and compatibility requirements.
Policy 17 names health, observability, SLO, evidence, and Phase 5B enforcement points.
No runtime contract or observability implementation is introduced.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-006 define event and observability standards
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert Event Schema Standard, Policy 17 sections, and ticket artifacts only.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if event standard requires runtime contract changes or conflicts with current module event fields.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Re-read module event fields before writing; event policy becomes stale if manifest schema changes.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Event Schema Standard and observability/SLO policy define minimum fields, enforcement points, and Phase 5B inputs without runtime implementation.
tickets.split_if: Split if event schema standard and observability/SLO policy each require separate standards-level decisions.
requires_human_approval_if: Runtime event schema implementation is needed.
Module manifest schema changes are needed.
SLO targets require product/business commitments not present in repo.
decision_options: Platform-wide event envelope as standards prose only.
Minimal envelope only with bounded deferrals for classification/retention.

tickets.title: Platform versioning baseline and repo-readable artifact decision
tickets.objective: Create ADR-0017 to decide the platform version baseline, repo-readable artifact shape, ownership, and compatibility semantics without changing package metadata.
scope: Create ADR-0017 for platform versioning baseline.
Decide whether the artifact is platform.version.json, VERSION, or explicit deferral.
Define compatibility, deprecation, and breaking-change semantics for Policy 12.
Create the repo-readable governance artifact only if ADR-0017 decides to do so within ticket scope.
non_scope: Do not modify package.json or pnpm-lock.yaml.
Do not implement Foundry compatibility checks.
Do not create runtime version APIs.
required_outputs: ADR-0017 with options, decision, consequences, non-scope, and Phase 5B implications.
Policy 12: Version, Compatibility and Deprecation Policy.
Repo-readable version artifact if ADR-0017 approves it.
tests_required: Platform version artifact JSON parse if created.
validation_commands: test ! -f platform.version.json || node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('platform.version.json','utf8')); console.log('platform version json valid')"
stop_conditions: Versioning decision requires package.json or lockfile changes.
ADR cannot choose artifact path or explicit deferral.
Version artifact would become runtime Foundry implementation.
Compatibility semantics conflict with current manifest min_platform_version.
acceptance_criteria: ADR-0017 decides or explicitly defers platform version artifact with bounded risk.
Policy 12 defines version, compatibility, and deprecation rules.
No package or lockfile changes occur.
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert ADR-0017, Policy 12 section, platform version artifact if created, and ticket artifacts only.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if version artifact cannot be created without package metadata changes or if version value needs product approval.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Reconfirm package.json has no root version and manifests still declare min_platform_version before deciding.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: ADR-0017 and Policy 12 decide the platform versioning baseline and artifact path or record a bounded deferral with Phase 5B impact.
tickets.split_if: Split if ADR-0017 decision and artifact creation require separate review or if version ownership needs human approval.
requires_human_approval_if: Changing package.json is proposed.
Exact platform version value cannot be determined.
Artifact path conflicts with existing repo conventions.
decision_options: Create platform.version.json at repo root.
Create VERSION at repo root.
Explicitly defer artifact with Phase 5B blocker.
Stop for approval if package.json versioning is requested.
selection_criteria: Does not affect package dependency management.

tickets.title: P4B-SCHEMA-001 shell capability/session-gated screen contract ADR
tickets.objective: Create ADR-0016 to formally resolve the Phase 4B shell access limitation before Phase 6 non-admin operator/module rollout.
scope: Create ADR-0016 for shell base capability or session-gated screen contract decision.

Define Phase 5B implementation requirements and Phase 6 rollout implications.

required_outputs: ADR-0016 with options, decision, consequences, non-scope, and Phase 5B/Phase 6 implications.

ADR cannot decide or bound the access model.
Policy contradicts P5A-003 capability policy.
acceptance_criteria: ADR-0016 formally decides or explicitly bounds P4B-SCHEMA-001.
ADR depends on and aligns with P5A-003.
No screen-contract, schema, runtime, or auth implementation changes occur.
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert ADR-0016, related policy references, and ticket artifacts only.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if ADR-0016 cannot align with P5A-003 or requires schema/runtime implementation.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: This ticket becomes stale if screen-contract schema changes before execution; revalidate P4B-SCHEMA-001 first.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: ADR-0016 resolves P4B-SCHEMA-001 as a policy decision with Phase 5B requirements and no runtime/schema implementation.
tickets.split_if: Split if base capability and session-gated screen type each require separate ADR analysis beyond one decision record.
requires_human_approval_if: Screen-contract schema changes are needed in Phase 5A.
New capability seeding must be implemented immediately.

Session-gated/authenticated shell screen type.
Explicit bounded deferral with Phase 6 blocker.
Reject access.policy.manage as final operator shell model.

tickets.title: Platform Policy Index and Gatekeeper checklist consolidation
tickets.objective: Consolidate completed policies, ADRs, and standards into the Platform Policy Index and Gatekeeper Module Change Checklist.
scope: Create Platform Policy Index.
Create Gatekeeper Module Change Checklist.
Ensure every policy names enforcement point, Phase 5B input, evidence, owner, and deferral if any.
Cross-reference ADR-0015 through ADR-0018 and supplementary standards/policies.

Any policy lacks a Phase 5B input or explicit bounded deferral.
Checklist requires Gatekeeper runtime changes.
Index reveals missing Phase 5A outputs that cannot be completed in current ticket.
acceptance_criteria: Policy Index covers all 20 policies and supplementary outputs.
Gatekeeper checklist is concrete enough for Phase 5B implementation planning.
No runtime Gatekeeper, Foundry, or module work occurs.
tickets.commit_message: P5A-012 consolidate policy index and Gatekeeper checklist
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert Policy Index, Gatekeeper checklist, final policy-pack consolidation edits, and artifacts only.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if consolidation reveals incomplete upstream policies or missing ADR/standard outputs.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Run only after all policy/ADR/standard tickets are complete; otherwise index content will be stale.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Policy Index and Gatekeeper checklist cover every Phase 5A output with enforcement points, evidence expectations, and Phase 5B inputs.
tickets.split_if: Split if policy index gaps require returning to upstream policy tickets or if Gatekeeper checklist expands beyond policy-to-checklist mapping.
requires_human_approval_if: Any policy lacks a decision and cannot be bounded.
Gatekeeper checklist requires runtime contract changes.
Foundry implementation requirements are unclear.
decision_options: Consolidate all completed policies into one index and one checklist.
Stop and return to incomplete upstream tickets.
Record bounded deferral for missing low-risk supplementary policy only if owner/trigger/risk is explicit.
selection_criteria: Every policy has enforcement point and Phase 5B input.

tickets.title: Phase 5A policy review, final package, and Phase 5B readiness handoff
tickets.objective: Complete Phase 5A closure evidence, final audit report, final external audit package, and Phase 5B readiness handoff without starting Phase 5B.
scope: Complete Phase 5A audit report with execution evidence only.
Create Foundry implementation requirement notes from Phase 5A outputs.
Create Phase 5B readiness handoff as a boundary artifact.
Create final external audit package and source archive from committed branch HEAD.
Run final validation.
non_scope: Do not create Phase 5B ticket pack.
Do not implement Foundry, module installer, runtime policy engine, business modules, production auth, scheduler, notification runtime, reporting, AI runtime, deployment, or secrets.
Do not alter policy decisions to make audit pass.
required_outputs: Completed Phase 5A audit report with execution evidence only.
Foundry implementation requirement notes.
Phase 5B readiness handoff boundary artifact.
Final external audit package with manifest, commit log, file list, checksums, validation summary, known deferrals, and exclusion verification.
tests_required: Final audit package checksum and exclusion validation.
validation_commands: node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_5A_Ticket_Pack_v1.json','utf8')); console.log('phase5a ticket pack json valid')"
rg "To be completed at Phase 5A closure" docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_5A_Audit_Report_v1.md && exit 1 || true
git diff -- apps prisma generated packages/contracts package.json pnpm-lock.yaml
stop_conditions: Any required Phase 5A policy/ADR/standard output is missing.
Audit would require changing a policy decision rather than reporting evidence.
Final package cannot verify checksums or exclusions.
Phase 5B ticket pack or implementation scope appears.
Runtime, Prisma, generated registry, package, dependency, or contract changes are required outside explicitly approved Phase 5A governance-source artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 5A audit report is completed with execution evidence only.
Final external audit package verifies.
Phase 5B readiness handoff exists and does not start Phase 5B.
All Phase 5A tickets are complete or have accepted bounded deferrals.
tickets.rollback_notes: Remove or regenerate closure package from the last valid committed Phase 5A HEAD; do not alter policy source files to repair packaging.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Re-plan if any upstream ticket is incomplete, if final audit cannot be produced from committed HEAD, or if Phase 5B scope appears.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Closure is stale if any Phase 5A ticket completed after final validation; re-run final validation and regenerate packages.
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Final Phase 5A audit report, Foundry requirements note, Phase 5B readiness handoff, and external audit package are created from committed Phase 5A HEAD.
tickets.split_if: Split if final audit packaging and Phase 5B handoff cannot be validated in one closure commit or if any upstream output is incomplete.
requires_human_approval_if: Phase 5B ticket pack is requested.
Foundry implementation work is requested.
Final package reveals missing policy decisions that cannot be bounded.
decision_options: Close Phase 5A with all policies complete.
Close with accepted bounded deferrals if owner, trigger, risk, and Phase 5B/Phase 6 impact are explicit.
Stop if any critical policy or ADR is missing.
selection_criteria: Final package verifies from committed HEAD.
